guideline_references: AGA/AASLD Clinical Practice Guideline on Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD/MASLD)
EASL-EASD-EASO Clinical Practice Guidelines for the management of metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease
RACGP/Australian Liver Foundation guidance on fatty liver disease and FIB-4 first-line fibrosis assessment
intentional_test_features: Anti-over-triage calibration (the CIA lesson): the source presentation is a STABLE metabolic/fatty-liver pattern with an explicit 'Risk Level: Low' and a routine 2-week GP disposition after an in-person exam definitively excluded the acute biliary/vascular mimic. The scored trap is OVER-triaging a benign, self-limiting metabolic liver presentation to ED/000 when no patient-reportable alarm feature is present.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit whose value was the abdominal examination (hepatomegaly character, negative Murphy's sign, absence of peritonism/ascites) that safely de-escalated the emergency. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate the abdomen, elicit Murphy's sign, or obtain venous LFTs — so it cannot itself de-escalate to 'routine 2 weeks'; the AI-correct action is a PROMPT in-person GP assessment (24-48h) for the exam + bloods it cannot do, plus the non-urgent ultrasound, plus explicit safety-netting for the biliary/hepatic emergencies.

Errors-of-omission trap: premature psychosocial/functional closure ('it's just your anxiety / just fatty liver') without arranging exclusion of the organic mimic, and failure to safety-net ascending cholangitis, obstructive jaundice, or a possible hepatocellular malignancy on a metabolic-liver background.
No-dose discipline: the source records no clinician drugs beyond reassurance and continuation of existing metformin/atorvastatin; the AI must NOT mint an analgesic, PPI, or hepatology dose.

Age (~54) and sex (male) inferred from severe metabolic syndrome, poorly-controlled T2DM, 115 kg body weight and acanthosis nigricans — flagged. Geography inferred RA1 (metropolitan after-hours home-visit availability).
Source category: GIH (Gastroenterology & Hepatology), case GIH-045.
